name: Mux Video
description: Mux Video — managed video infrastructure. Ingest source URLs as Assets, get HLS playback URLs back, request MP4 static renditions, run live streams. Strong on transcode + delivery + thumbnails; this integration covers the Video v1 API surface most useful as a render executor for the media app.

credential_fields.name: username
credential_fields.label: Access Token ID
credential_fields.description: Mux Access Token ID — issued from the Settings → Access Tokens page. Acts as the username for HTTP Basic auth.

credential_fields.name: password
credential_fields.label: Secret Key
credential_fields.description: Paired secret. Acts as the password for HTTP Basic auth.

tools.name: create_asset
tools.description: Create a new asset by ingesting one or more source URLs. Mux fetches the input(s), transcodes them, and exposes HLS via playback_ids. Returns asset id + initial state (`preparing`); poll get_asset until ready/errored. To enable downloadable MP4s, pass `mp4_support: capped-1080p` (or `audio-only`).

inputs.description: Array of input objects, each at minimum {url}. Single-input is the common case; multi-input is for stitching subtitle + audio + video tracks. Mux's deprecated `input` (singular) alias still works but new code should use `inputs`.

url.description: HTTPS URL Mux can fetch the source from.

playback_policies.description: Playback access modes. Use ["public"] for unsigned playback, ["signed"] when you need per-viewer JWTs. Required for the asset to be playable.

video_quality.description: Replaces the deprecated `encoding_tier`. `basic` is cheapest single-rendition; `plus`/`premium` enable adaptive bitrate ladders.

mp4_support.description: Generate downloadable MP4 renditions alongside HLS. Useful when you want a single-file output (matches our render contract) instead of streaming.

passthrough.description: Free-form metadata Mux echoes back in webhooks. Good for round-tripping our render_id.

test.description: Asset is purged after 24h; useful for development without burning storage.

tools.name: list_assets
tools.description: Paginate through assets. Useful for sync or housekeeping.

tools.name: get_asset
tools.description: Fetch one asset's metadata + state. Poll this after create_asset until status=`ready` (or `errored`).

tools.name: delete_asset
tools.description: Permanently delete an asset and stop billing for its storage.

tools.name: create_playback_id
tools.description: Mint an additional playback id on an asset — useful for revoking access to one URL while keeping others alive (signed mode).

tools.name: create_static_rendition
tools.description: Request an MP4 static rendition of an asset at a specific resolution. Useful as a render-executor primitive: the asset = source, the rendition = output mp4. Poll the asset to see when the rendition is ready (it appears under `static_renditions.files`).

tools.name: create_direct_upload
tools.description: Provision a one-time signed PUT URL the client can use to upload bytes directly to Mux's storage, bypassing your server. Returns {url, asset_id}; client uploads to `url`, Mux ingests into `asset_id`.

new_asset_settings.description: Same shape as create_asset's body (minus `inputs`, since the upload IS the input). At minimum needs `playback_policies`.

cors_origin.description: Origin allowed to PUT. Use "*" to allow any browser; pin to your domain in production.

timeout.description: Seconds the upload URL stays valid.

tools.name: create_live_stream
tools.description: Create a live stream endpoint. Returns a `stream_key` for the broadcaster + `playback_ids` for viewers. Out of scope for media's render executor needs but useful when this integration is bound for live-streaming workflows.

new_asset_settings.description: Settings for the recorded asset Mux creates from the stream.

reconnect_window.description: Seconds Mux waits for the stream to come back before ending it. 0 disables.
